content: macOS 26 SDK declares SwiftUI @State as an attached macro whose SwiftUIMacros implementation plugin ships only in full Xcode, not standalone Command Line Tools; CLT swift build fails with 'external macro implementation type could not be found'. typealias VPState=SwiftUI.State references the property-wrapper TYPE, sidestepping macro-name resolution. Fix: build with the Xcode 26 toolchain (Xcode app, xcodebuild, or xcrun swift build) makes the plugin available so real @State compiles; the VPState alias then becomes unnecessary but harmless and can be kept for CLT dev.
